rationale.edition: All 21 changed paths are under web/src/ or tests/ui-testing/ — pure OSS directories. No enterprise/ or o2_enterprise/ paths appear anywhere in the diff. No SSO, SAML, RBAC, SDR, or other enterprise keywords in the changed file paths or symbol names.
rationale.skip: The diff is not skipped. The change is user-facing: it alters which column the logs table renders by default (FTS body field instead of generic 'source'). The trigger_comment and feature_hint are both empty — no explicit skip marker or tests-added label. This is not a docs-only or CI-only change.
rationale.e2e: E2E is warranted. The FTS default-column feature changes what users see in the logs results table — the first column is now a filled FTS field (body/message/log) instead of always 'source'. This affects table rendering, row-detail clicks, severity-color extraction, and column persistence. The feature interacts with quick mode, SQL mode, and stream switching — all user-observable behaviors that Playwright can validate.
rationale.area: All UI changes are in the Logs plugin (web/src/plugins/logs/) and its composables (web/src/composables/useLogs/). The three modified Playwright E2E specs belong to the Logs folder (tests/ui-testing/playwright-tests/Logs/).
rationale.group: Logs-Core is the best fit. The feature changes core table rendering behavior, and the most closely related modified spec (logstable.spec.js — severity color mapping in the results table) lives in Logs-Core. The other two modified specs span Logs-Features and Logs-Builder-Advanced, but the core default-column rendering logic is a foundational Logs behavior.
rationale.existing_tests: Three Playwright E2E specs were modified in the diff: logstable.spec.js (Logs-Core), indexquery.spec.js (Logs-Features), and logsquickmode.spec.js (Logs-Builder-Advanced). These existing tests were updated to accommodate the FTS default-column change (relaxed selectors, changed assertions) but do not add dedicated coverage for the feature's behavior (default-column selection logic, fill-rate priority, persistence guard, SQL-mode bypass). The diff also includes Vitest unit tests (useLogs.spec.ts, useSearchBar.spec.ts, SearchResult.spec.ts) and POM page-object changes — these are not E2E specs and do not count as existing E2E coverage.
